document.heading: Contribution of the Upper Mattaponi Tribe Flag
document.text: A Resolution to affirm the Upper Mattaponi Tribe's status as a federally recognized tribe through the contribution of its flag to the Hall of Tribal Nations.
At a duly called General Meeting of the Upper Mattaponi Tribe Council held this 24th day of January 2026, the following Resolution and Law were adopted.

recital.text: the Upper Mattaponi Tribe (the "Tribe") is an Indian Tribe that has existed from time immemorial and is federally recognized on a government-to-government basis by the United States of America; and

Pursuant to Article VII, Section 4(c) of the Constitution of the Upper Mattaponi Tribe (the "Constitution"), the Upper Mattaponi Tribe Council (the "Council") is vested with the authority to enact laws and resolutions for the benefit, governance, and representation of the Tribe; and

the Tribe is a sovereign nation with the inherent authority and responsibility to preserve, protect, and promote its political status, cultural identity, history, and symbols of nationhood for present and future generations; and
the Tribal flag and Tribal seal are a symbol of the Tribe's sovereignty, self-determination, unity, and enduring presence as a distinct Indigenous nation; and

the Bureau of Indian Affairs Museum's Hall of Tribal Nations serves as a national and educational space honoring federally recognized Tribes by displaying their flags as expressions of sovereignty, nationhood, and government-to-government relationships with the United States; and

the contribution of the Upper Mattaponi Tribe's flag to the Hall of Tribal Nations will ensure that the Tribe is visibly and respectfully represented alongside other sovereign Tribal Nations, affirming the Tribe's rightful place within the federal trust relationship and the broader history of Indigenous nations; and

such representation promotes awareness, education, and respect for the Upper Mattaponi Tribe's history, culture, and continued existence, and serves as a point of pride for Tribal citizens and future generations; and
the Bureau of Indian Affairs requires a duly adopted Tribal Resolution authorizing the contribution of a Tribal flag to the Hall of Tribal Nations;

THAT the Upper Mattaponi Tribal Council authorizes the contribution of the official flag of the Upper Mattaponi Tribe to the Hall of Tribal Nations at the Bureau of Indian Affairs Museum, affirming the Tribe's federally recognized status, sovereignty, and government-to-government relationship with the United States. The Chief of the Upper Mattaponi Tribe, or their designee, is authorized to take all actions necessary to carry out this Resolution, which shall take effect immediately upon adoption.

procedural-data.text: The foregoing Resolution was duly voted upon by the Council on the 24th day of January, 2026, at a General Meeting with a vote of 7 in favor and 0 opposed, 0 abstaining, and 0 absent, pursuant to the authority vested in the Council by the Constitution of the Upper Mattaponi Tribe.
